title: Design accountable and conflict-safe collective mutations
goal: Define and implement a measured safety layer for collective node changes: stale-write detection, actor-bound audit evidence, reversible deletion policy, abuse controls and mutation observability, without weakening the immediate shared-edit product model.

acceptance.assertion: A documented API contract chooses and tests optimistic concurrency for replace and patch, such as ETag/If-Match or an exact updated_at precondition, including deterministic 409 or 412 behavior and a usable frontend conflict path.
acceptance.verifier: API contract, PostgreSQL and JSONL race tests, browser conflict test

acceptance.assertion: Every collective replace and delete emits durable actor-bound evidence containing operation type, target id, timestamp and privacy-reviewed before/after hashes or redacted deltas; the audit record cannot be confused with public node content.
acceptance.verifier: schema, privacy review, persistence and restart tests

acceptance.assertion: Soft delete, tombstones, bounded undo or permanent delete is selected through an explicit product and data-retention decision; recovery and purge behavior are tested before public UI exposure.
Account-scoped replace/delete rate limits and administrative emergency behavior are threat-modeled, configurable and tested without reusing authentication-email buckets.
acceptance.verifier: threat model, configuration contract and rate-limit tests

acceptance.assertion: Mutation latency, failure class, conflict count and JSONL recovery activity are measurable without leaking node contents or account identifiers.
acceptance.verifier: metrics contract and privacy-safe telemetry tests

metadata.scope_decision: These changes alter API and product semantics and therefore do not block the already integrity-hardened PR #1440.
metadata.preserved_product_rule: All authenticated Weber may continue to edit and delete shared nodes immediately; this task adds accountability and conflict handling rather than ownership restrictions.
